memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of William Lawrence and Katerin Beaumont in "The Thomas Book," page 398.
memoryArtifacts.description:
This source seems to be the origin of information for this family, at least in printed form.  
Lawrence Buckley Thomas, "The Thomas Book," (New York: Trow Print, 1896), 398.  This work distinguishes many different families.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Lawrence於1523年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯時，他的父親John Edward Lawrence21歲，母親Margaret Kaye13歲。他於1559年11月25日與Katherine Neville Beaumont在英國賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他去世於1584年享年61歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯。

nameMeanings.name: Lawrence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

variants.name: Larsson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Erik, Anders, Fredrik, Nels, Nils, Olle, Per, Sten, Algot, Birgit, Bjorn.  German Bernhard, Hans, Markus, Oskar.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence . Compare Larson 1, Larrison , and Lawson 2.
Americanized form of Norwegian and rare North German or Danish patronymic Larssen, a cognate of 1 above. Compare Larsen and Larson 1.

variants.name: Laurance
meanings: English: variant of   Lawrence  .

variants.name: Lafrenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Juergen, Arno, Hans, Otto.
German: from a variant of the personal name Laurenz (see Lawrence ).

variants.name: Law
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from a Middle English personal name Law(e), a short form of Lawrence .

Scottish and northern English: topographic name for someone who lived near a hill, northern Middle English law (from Old English hlāw, which generally became low in the south and law in the north). Compare Lowe and Lew .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 羅, based on its Cantonese pronunciation, see   Luo   1.

variants.name: Larsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Nels, Niels, Sven, Thor, Knud, Nils, Per, Jorgen, Alf, Bjorn.

Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence . This is also an Americanized form of the cognate Larssen. Compare Larson 1, Larrison , and Lawson 2.

variants.name: Lassen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Svein, Sven, Thor, Hilmar, Lasse, Niels.  German Hans, Lorenz, Erwin, Manfred, Otto, Uwe.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from Lasse, a pet form of Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence .
North German: possibly also an assimilated form of Danish Larssen (see Larsen ).

variants.name: Larrance
meanings: Altered form of English   Lawrence  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

variants.name: Larry
meanings: English: from the personal name Larry, a pet form of Lawrence .

variants.name: Larkin
meanings: English: from a medieval personal name, a diminutive of Lar-, Lor-, both pet forms of Lawrence , formed with the addition of the Middle English suffix -kin.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Lorcáin ‘descendant of Lorcán’, a personal name from a diminutive of lorc ‘fierce, cruel’. This Gaelic name was often regarded as an equivalent of English Lawrence .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When William Lawrence was born in 1523, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Edward Lawrence, was 21 and his mother, Margaret Kaye, was 13. He married Katherine Neville Beaumont on 25 November 1559, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 8 daughters. He died in 1584, at the age of 61, and was buried in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Lawrence est né en 1523, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Edward Lawrence, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret Kaye, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Neville Beaumont le 25 novembre 1559, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 8 filles. Il est décédé en 1584, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Lawrence 1523 in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Edward Lawrence 21 und seine Mutter Margaret Kaye 13. Er hat am 25. November 1559 in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England Katherine Neville Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er starb 1584 im Alter von 61 Jahren und wurde in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Lawrence è nato il nel 1523 nel St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Edward Lawrence, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Kaye, aveva 13 anni. Lui sposato Katherine Neville Beaumont il 25 novembre 1559, nel St Albans, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 8 figlie. Morì nel 1584, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a St Albans, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Lawrenceが1523年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスで生まれた時，父John Edward Lawrenceは21歳，母Margaret Kayeは13歳であった。1559年11月25日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスでKatherine Neville Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1584年に61歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Lawrence(이)가 1523년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Edward Lawrence(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Kaye(은)는 13세였다. 그는 1559년 11월 25일 영국 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 Katherine Neville Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1584년 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Lawrence nasceu em 1523, em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Edward Lawrence, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Kaye, tinha 13 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Neville Beaumont em 25 de novembro de 1559, em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1584, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Lawrence родился в 1523 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Edward Lawrence было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Kaye, 13. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Neville Beaumont 25 ноября 1559 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Он умер в 1584 г. в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Lawrence nació en 1523, en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Edward Lawrence, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margaret Kaye, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Katherine Neville Beaumont el 25 de noviembre de 1559, en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió en 1584, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en St Albans, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
